en: A developer is onboarding and has a quick definitional question
Someone is mid-task and just needs a fast clarification
A student learning to code needs a basic concept explained
A developer quickly checks a fact before continuing work
Someone is half-reading documentation and needs a term clarified
ko: 개발 입문자가 기초 개념을 빠르게 확인하려는 상황
코딩 중 용어가 헷갈려서 잠깐 확인하는 상황
온보딩 중에 가벼운 질문을 하는 상황
문서 읽다가 모르는 단어 나온 상황
팀원한테 물어보기 애매해서 AI한테 물어보는 상황

confusion_patterns: 'what is X' can look like reasoning if X is complex — but simple class means the answer is a single sentence lookup, no analysis
'show me X' could be agentic if X requires building something, but simple if X is just a definition

imperative: explain why this function is slow
review this code and point out issues
explain how async/await works in javascript
analyze this SQL query for performance issues
explain the tradeoffs between a list and a linked list
explain how the rust borrow checker works
explain when to use a hashmap vs a binary search tree
casual: can you take a look at this and tell me what's off
do you see anything weird in this function
hey walk me through why this would fail
what do you think about this approach
is this the right way to do it or am I overcomplicating it
can you break down what this code is actually doing
why does this pattern cause memory leaks
how would you approach this differently
frustrated: I don't understand why this is failing, can you just explain it
this makes no sense, explain it to me like I'm five
ugh why does async/await still confuse me, help

uncertain: I think my approach is okay but I'm not sure — can you review it

does this make sense or am I missing something obvious
is this considered good practice or should I rethink it

my team keeps telling me my error handling is wrong but I don't see it
users are reporting slow responses but I can't find the bottleneck
the code works but somehow it feels wrong

en: A developer is stuck understanding why their code behaves unexpectedly and needs a clear explanation
A senior dev is doing a design review before committing to an approach
Someone is preparing for a code review and wants a second opinion
A developer is learning a new language feature and needs it explained with context
A team lead is trying to understand tradeoffs before making an architectural decision
ko: 코드가 왜 이렇게 동작하는지 이해가 안 돼서 설명이 필요한 상황
접근 방식 확정 전에 설계 검토하는 상황
코드 리뷰 전에 미리 점검하는 상황
새로운 언어 기능 배우면서 맥락과 함께 이해하려는 상황
아키텍처 결정 전 트레이드오프 분석하는 상황

en: I've been looking at this for a while and [INTENT]
before I commit to this approach, [INTENT]

confusion_patterns: 'explain X' overlaps with simple — key: reasoning requires analysis or evaluation, not just a lookup

key_signals: analysis only — produce insight not artifacts
confusion_patterns: 'fix this bug' = agentic (do it), 'explain why this is a bug' = reasoning (analyze it)

imperative: fix the bug in the auth module
add error handling to all API calls
refactor this function to be more readable
write unit tests for the user service
migrate this codebase from callbacks to async/await
find and fix all the N+1 queries in this project
set up a CI pipeline for this repo
implement pagination for the users endpoint
rename all instances of userId to user_id throughout the project
add input validation to all form handlers

this endpoint is throwing 500s in production
the build keeps failing on the CI server

en: A developer has identified a bug in production and needs it fixed now
A team lead is delegating a well-scoped ticket to an AI agent
A developer is starting a new feature and wants the scaffolding set up
An on-call engineer needs automated help to investigate and fix an incident
A developer is doing cleanup work before a big release
ko: 프로덕션 버그가 발생해서 지금 바로 고쳐야 하는 상황
팀 리드가 잘 정의된 태스크를 AI에게 위임하는 상황
새 기능 시작 전에 스캐폴딩을 세팅하는 상황
온콜 엔지니어가 인시던트를 자동으로 조사하고 수정해야 하는 상황
큰 릴리즈 전에 정리 작업을 하는 상황

confusion_patterns: 'add X' could seem simple but agentic means actually doing it, not explaining it
'explain how to fix X' = reasoning, 'fix X' = agentic — the key is whether execution is required

en: while you're doing [INTENT_A], can you also [INTENT_B]
[INTENT_A] — oh and [INTENT_B] while you're at it
can you [INTENT_A] and when you're done [INTENT_B]

typo_rules: common keyboard adjacency swaps: e→r, i→o, s→a, n→m
missing space between words with 1% probability
